scope.summary: Expose air_drag as a first-class field in FabricProperties LLM surface and MaterialParamsGpu so models can author flutter behavior and the WGSL aerodynamics pass reads it per-material rather than using a global constant
acceptance_criteria: FabricProperties in tailor-solver/src/spec.rs MUST include air_drag: f32 with range [0.0, 1.0] and a doc comment explaining 0.0 = no drag, 1.0 = maximal flutter resistance (equivalent to dense still-air damping for chiffon-weight fabrics)
MaterialParamsGpu in tailor-solver/src/gpu/material_uniform.rs MUST include air_drag: f32; the padding field _pad MUST be adjusted or relocated to maintain 16-byte alignment

The WGSL integrate shader (predict.wgsl or equivalent) MUST read air_drag from the MaterialParams uniform and apply it as a velocity-proportional drag force on each particle: v_new = v * (1.0 - air_drag * dt) before gravity integration
The logarithmic mapping in material_decoder MUST NOT be applied to air_drag (it is already a dimensionless rate coefficient, not a compliance); it MUST be passed through linearly after a [0,1] clamp

MT-199 system preset calibrations MUST be updated to include air_drag values: chiffon >= 0.05, silk >= 0.02, cotton >= 0.01, denim >= 0.005, leather <= 0.003, consistent with the existing physics_json values
The FABRIC_PROPERTIES_IN_RANGE ValidationDescriptor MUST include air_drag in its range check ([0.0, 1.0])

The fabric integration test MUST assert that a chiffon panel with air_drag=0.05 and a leather panel with air_drag=0.003 subject to the same wind field at the same frame produce measurably different lateral displacement
scope.risk_if_missed: MT-206 implements aerodynamic air-drag as a module but if air_drag is not in FabricProperties it cannot be set per-preset or per-panel by a model or operator. The MaterialParamsGpu struct in the spec (spec line ~2397) does not list air_drag, meaning the GPU shader cannot read it from the material uniform. The result is that all fabrics share the same aerodynamic behavior regardless of weight — chiffon and leather flutter identically — which is a production-visible defect for any wind-affected animation. ClothMaterialPhysics has the field but it is not exposed through the two-layer API, making the field unreachable from model and operator authoring paths.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
